name: Jupiter Conjunct Natal Sun — Fame Activation Transit
description: Transit Jupiter conjunct natal Sun is the single strongest transit for fame activation. Expands public visibility, authority, and recognition from superiors.

source_text.quote: When Jupiter transits over the natal Sun, the native receives honor from the king, gains authority, and becomes famous in the land.

all_of: Transit Jupiter is within 5 degrees of natal Sun longitude

interpretation.meaning: Jupiter (expansion, dharma, grace) passing over natal Sun (self, fame, authority) activates the fame potential. Awards, promotions, public honors, government recognition typically manifest.

metadata.notes: The single most reliable fame transit. 12-year cycle. Each occurrence marks a potential fame peak.

name: Jupiter Return — Dharma Recognition Cycle
description: Jupiter returning to its natal position (every ~12 years) activates wisdom-fame, spiritual recognition, and dharmic honor. Amplified if natal Jupiter is strong.

source_text.quote: When Jupiter returns to its natal position, the fruits of past dharmic actions ripen, bringing recognition, honor, and expansion of influence.

all_of: Transit Jupiter is within 5 degrees of natal Jupiter longitude

interpretation.meaning: Jupiter Return marks a 12-year dharmic cycle. Past wisdom, teaching, and righteous action bear fruit as public recognition, spiritual authority, or institutional honor.

metadata.notes: Occurs at ages ~12, 24, 36, 48, 60. Each return amplifies if natal Jupiter is well-placed.

name: Abhishek Tara (28th Nakshatra) Transit — Coronation Window
description: When slow planets (Jupiter/Saturn) transit the 28th nakshatra from birth Moon (Abhishek Tara = coronation star), a fame/recognition window activates.

source_text.quote: The 28th nakshatra from birth star is Abhishek Tara, the star of coronation and anointing. Transits over this point bring public elevation.

all_of: Transit Jupiter or Saturn is in the 28th nakshatra from birth Moon nakshatra

interpretation.meaning: Abhishek Tara (28th nakshatra from Moon) is literally the 'coronation star'. Slow planet transits here create windows for public elevation, awards, titles, and formal recognition.

metadata.notes: Abhishek = coronation/anointing. This tara system is specific to Vedic electional astrology applied to natal charts.

name: Benefics Transit 10th House — Public Action Window
description: When multiple natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury) transit the 10th house from lagna simultaneously, a strong public visibility and recognition window opens.

source_text.quote: When benefic planets transit the 10th house, the native gains in profession, receives public honor, and their good deeds become known to all.

all_of: At least 2 natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, Moon) are transiting the 10th house from Lagna

interpretation.meaning: Multiple benefics in the 10th house create a powerful positive glow on public reputation. Professional honors, media attention, and career-based fame manifest.

metadata.notes: Strongest when Jupiter is one of the benefics. Venus alone gives artistic/beauty recognition.
